I'll help you list the unresolved Sentry issues in the 'billing' project and count them. First, let me check the structure of the sentry_issues table to understand what columns are available.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'sentry_issues'"}

text: Now I can query for unresolved Sentry issues in the 'billing' project. I'll get both the IDs and count them in one query.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, title, level, count, status FROM sentry_issues WHERE project = 'billing' AND status = 'unresolved' ORDER BY id"}

text: Now let me count them as well to provide the exact number:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) as unresolved_count FROM sentry_issues WHERE project = 'billing' AND status = 'unresolved'"}
